dialogues.text: Upon arrival, Luna finds that Aguinaldo is not present, raising doubts and confusion. The atmosphere feels tense, and Luna senses that something is wrong—but it is already too late.
Where is the President? This telegram was urgent. The air here feels wrong.
(Luna enters the convent and encounters members of the Kawit Battalion. The Commander approaches.)
General Luna. The President is not here. We are in charge of the post.
In charge? I received a direct summons from Aguinaldo! Where is he, and why are you men of the Kawit Battalion here? You should be fighting the Americans, not idling at headquarters!
Watch your tone, General. Your orders do not apply here.
(A heated confrontation quickly escalates into violence.)
Outnumbered and unprepared, Luna fights back fiercely but is ultimately overwhelmed.
Cowards! You fall upon your own General? Are you truly so blind that you cannot see the real enemy?
(Luna is stabbed and shot repeatedly by his fellow Filipino soldiers. He falls.)
Traitors… not to foreign enemies… but to my own countrymen…
His death sends shockwaves through the already fragile army.
